name: Sling Scheduling API
what_it_does: Employee scheduling and team communication platform with REST API — combining shift scheduling, time clock, task management, and team messaging for retail, food service, and hospitality with a free tier for unlimited employees.
use_cases: Creating and publishing shift schedules for hourly employees via API
Syncing time clock data to payroll for hours worked calculation
Querying labor cost data by location and position for budget tracking
Managing employee availability and time-off requests programmatically
Integrating Sling scheduling data with POS for labor optimization
not_for: Knowledge worker scheduling or project-based resource planning
Enterprise workforce management with complex compliance (use Kronos or ADP)
best_when: A small-to-mid-size business with hourly workers needs free or affordable shift scheduling combined with team messaging and task management.
avoid_when: You need complex labor forecasting, multi-location enterprise analytics, or salaried employee management.

auth.notes: API token authentication. Login endpoint returns session token. No OAuth2 — proprietary token system. Account-level access. Token included in Authorization header. Limited public API documentation.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: unlimited employees, basic scheduling and messaging
pricing.notes: Generous free tier with unlimited employees. Very affordable paid plans. US-based. Acquired by Toast (restaurant POS) in 2019. Strong Toast ecosystem integration. Well-suited for restaurant and food service industry. API documentation limited compared to competitors.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits apply; not publicly documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for shifts, users, positions, and time clock. Non-standard token auth (login-based, not OAuth2). No webhooks. Limited public API documentation — less agent-friendly than When I Work or Deputy. Toast acquisition may mean future POS-first integration focus.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Shift creation creates new shifts. User management idempotent by ID. Time punch records append-only. Read operations idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Limited public API documentation — may need to reverse-engineer from app
Login-based token auth — not standard OAuth2
Toast acquisition means API development may be Toast-POS focused
No webhooks — polling required for real-time data

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. US data residency. Login-based token auth (weaker than OAuth2). No scope granularity. Toast POS ecosystem integration. Standard security for SMB scheduling tool.
